raw_consumer_statement:
Please see below letter to Citi Customer Service - Subject : Fraudulent Charge Dispute Citi XXXX XXXX Account Ending in XXXX Dear Citi Customer Service, I am writing to formally dispute a fraudulent charge on my Citi XXXX XXXX  account ending in XXXX. I noticed this unauthorized charge on my statement dated XX/XX/XXXXXXXX  and want to ensure this issue is resolved promptly. 

Fraudulent Charge Details : Merchant Name : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX CAN Transaction Date : XX/XX/XXXX Charge Amount : {$1300.00} This charge was not authorized by me or any authorized users on my account. I have no prior relationship with this merchant and did not make or benefit from this transaction. I have proactively taken steps to secure my account, including reporting this matter to your fraud department on XX/XX/XXXX. Despite my efforts, the issue remains unresolved after eight follow-up calls. My most recent calls were on XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. 

During these conversations, I was informed that while the charge is noted as disputed, representatives are unable to process the dispute because the transaction was associated with a previous card number. This explanation is both confusing and frustrating, as I had expected Citi to provide a resolution regardless of the card 's status, given the fraudulent nature of the charge. 

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), I request that Citi : 1. Investigate and reverse the fraudulent charge.

2. Confirm in writing that my liability is limited to {$50.00} or waived entirely, as required by law.

If further information is needed, please do not hesitate to contact me at ( XXXX ) XXXX or XXXX. 

Thank you for your immediate attention to this matter. I look forward to resolving this promptly. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
